title: dryopteris impressa
intro: Dryopteris impressa, also known as the stiff-leaved wood fern or interrupted fern, is a species of fern native to Europe, North Africa, and western Asia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Dryopteris impressa include its ability to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its use in treating wounds and skin conditions. Dryopteris impressa can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
